original_thesis: The 'test before you commit' stack. Experimentation generates evidence; scientific method validates it; critical thinking evaluates it; antifragility benefits from the volatility of testing; agile provides the iteration cadence.
corrected_thesis: The 'test before you commit' stack. Metacognitive questioning asks whether your reasoning process is sound; experimentation generates evidence; scientific method structures the testing; critical thinking evaluates the results; causal-attribution-resistance prevents accepting the first plausible explanation; decomposition isolates variables; root-cause-analysis traces failures to their source; systems thinking reveals what the experiment can't capture in isolation.
what_this_stack_defeats: Theory without testing, premature commitment, unfalsifiable beliefs
notes: Original thesis mentioned antifragility and agile — not members. Corrected to describe actual members. Shares Causal Forensics trunk + experimentation and metacognitive-questioning as divergence points. The 'test first' framing is accurate for the expanded member set.
